pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i walk into the kitchen and i forget for a second that he isn't there, and then the silence just hits me all over again. it feels like i'm just going through the motions of a life i don't even belong to anymore. everything just feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope... and i couldn't stop thinking about how everything we do is just a tiny blip of noise in an infinite, freezing vacuum. it feels like we're all just performing this exhausting act of 'living' for no reason at all. like, if the end result is just entropy and heat death, why are we even bothering with the struggle?
pool.message: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope... and i couldn't stop thinking about how everything we do is just a tiny blip of noise in an infinite, freezing vacuum. it feels like we're all just performing this exhausting act of 'living' for no reason at all. like, if the end result is just entropy and heat death, why are we even bothering with the struggle?

pool.baseline: i just don't see the point anymore. three years of this research, the late nights, the funding falling through, and now the lab is basically shutting down. i feel like i've wasted my entire life on something that doesn't even matter. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting for a future that isn't even there.

pool.baseline: the candles are all burned down now... and there is nothing left but the cold. he is gone, and with him, everything that made this life worth enduring. i feel as though i am already a ghost, just waiting for the heavy velvet of the night to finally claim me and pull me into the silence. it would be so much easier to just... let the darkness take me, wouldn't it?

pool.baseline: it’s weird being out here. i can walk through these crowded streets for hours and feel like i’m literally a ghost. like, if i just stopped showing up tomorrow, the world wouldn't even glitch. it's just a zero-sum game. i take up space, i consume resources, but there's zero net impact on anything. i'm just... background noise.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the crash hit harder than anyone expected and the debt is just... it's insurmountable. i lost the company, the house, everything. i can't even imagine walking back into my parents' house or looking my wife in the eye. i'm just a complete failure. there's no coming back from this.

pool.baseline: it never stops. even when i'm asleep, the burning in my legs just feels like it carries over. i'm so tired of fighting just to exist in a body that feels like it's constantly on fire. i just want everything to be still. i just want the noise and the pain to finally go away.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A collapsing digital archive. The world is flickering, losing resolution, and dissolving into white noise. You are the last conscious entity left in this sector.]

Everything is just... fading. Every memory I have, every connection I thought mattered, it's all just turning into static. If the system wipes this entire sector tonight, like it's planned, does it even matter that I was here? Like, does anything actually stay? It feels so heavy, just trying to hold onto data that's already gone.
